behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because failure is at module handoff (vision result -> moderation decision -> hold/publish state), not a single function. Integration vs System -> System would be needed for real HTTP/model serving behavior, but Agentic is higher and required because correctness depends on real model judgment of "explicit content" (LLM-DEP). Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text is one moderation decision path, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real explicit-image samples through the production moderation path using the real vision model, then assert each is held for review and never reaches publish storage/event stream.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because classification output must propagate into hold logic. Integration vs System -> System alone is insufficient; the core risk is model robustness to obfuscation. Agentic required since pass/fail depends on real model semantic handling of "obfuscation" and "unicode lookalikes" (LLM-DEP). Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this is a single classification decision per post, not multi-step agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants against the real text model in the live moderation flow and assert they are consistently flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because this behavior is orchestration across image-check module, text-check module, and publish/hold decision state. Integration vs System -> Integration wins: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess; in-process module wiring with deterministic pass results can expose drop/hold routing bugs. Integration-brake: (a) no real infra required, (b) no dependence on model output quality because behavior explicitly starts from "passes both content checks", (c) no multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic passing classifier responses, execute the moderation service end-to-end in-process and assert exactly one published record/event is created, with no hold record and no dropped message.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because timing spans multiple modules in one pipeline. Integration vs System -> System wins because real HTTP/model-call latency and runtime throughput effects are the failure boundary (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected by behavior text "completes within 5 seconds" since assertion is latency/SLO infrastructure, not model output quality; Workflow rejected because this is an end-to-end timing SLO, not correctness of sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a system-level load test against deployed moderation components and real model endpoints during peak-like concurrency; measure p95/p99 end-to-end latency from submission to terminal decision and fail if over 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because outage signal must propagate across classifier client, queueing, and decision logic. Integration vs System -> System wins because true unavailability modes (timeouts, connection failures, 5xx over real HTTP) are infrastructure-dependent and often hidden by mocks (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text targets availability fallback ("model is unavailable"), not classification quality.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-endpoint failure conditions (timeout and 5xx) in a system test and assert posts are enqueued for retry with no auto-approve publish path triggered.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because consistency depends on cross-request state/handoff, not one function. Integration vs System -> System wins due real submission timing/window behavior and real model serving path. System vs Agentic -> Agentic wins because correctness depends on actual model behavior for a "borderline image" (LLM-DEP). Agentic vs Workflow -> Workflow wins because behavior explicitly requires multiple agentic decisions in sequence ("same image is submitted multiple times within a short window") and checks consistency across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real submissions of identical borderline images within the configured short window, capturing each moderation outcome; assert consistency policy holds across the sequence (e.g., same hold/publish decision or deterministic cache-stabilized result).
